title: Acceleration profile for sprinter detection
abstract: Example implementations include a method, apparatus and computer-readable medium for automated sprinter detection, comprising receiving one or more images from one or more image sensors associated with a security system. The implementations further include identifying a person in the one or more images. Additionally, the implementations further include calculating an acceleration of the person based on tracking of the person in the one or more images. Additionally, the implementations further include identifying the person as a sprinter based on the acceleration. Additionally, the implementations further include causing autonomous execution of one or more sprinter response actions in response to identifying the person as the sprinter.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method for automated sprinter detection, comprising: receiving one or more images from one or more image sensors associated with a security system; identifying a person in the one or more images; calculating an acceleration of the person based on tracking of the person in the one or more images; identifying the person as a sprinter based on the acceleration; and causing autonomous execution of one or more sprinter response actions in response to identifying the person as the sprinter. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein calculating the acceleration of the person further comprises: calculating a distance the person traveled based on the tracking of the person in the one or more images, wherein at least a subset of the one or more images are associated with different frames; and calculating a cumulative speed of the person for the different frames based on the distance traveled by the person, wherein the acceleration is calculated for the different frames based on the cumulative speed and a rolling frame interval. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the rolling frame interval is a predetermined number of frames. 4. The method of any one of claims 2 or 3, wherein the acceleration is a second order derivative of the cumulative speed of the person. 5. The method of any one of claims 2-4, wherein identifying the person as the sprinter based on the acceleration further comprises: identifying a number of peaks of the acceleration within a set of frames; and determining whether the number of peaks of the acceleration satisfies a threshold number of peaks, and wherein the person is identified based on the determination that the number of peaks satisfies the threshold number of peaks. 6. The method of claim 5, further comprising: dividing the different frames into different zones; identifying a number of zones of the different zones the person traveled through based on tracking of the person in one or more images associated with the different frames; and determining whether the number of zones satisfies a threshold number of zones, wherein the person is identified based further on the determination that the number of zones satisfies the threshold number of zones. 7. The method of any one of claims 1-6, wherein at least a subset of the one or more image sensors are located near an entry point or an exit point of a structure associated with the security system. 8. The method of any one of claims 1-7, wherein causing autonomous execution of at least one sprinter response action of the one or more sprinter response actions further comprises: transmitting an alert to at least one or more computing devices associated with the security system. 9. The method of any one of claims 1-8, wherein causing autonomous execution of at least one sprinter response action of the one or more sprinter response actions further comprises: transmitting an alert to one or more computing devices associated with a local security personnel or a law enforcement personnel. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein transmitting the alert further comprises: identifying the person within a person of interest database, wherein the alert indicates information from the person of interest database associated with the person. 11. The method of any one of claims 9 or 10, further comprising: storing information associated with tracking of the person in the one or more images in a data storage unit associated with security system. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the stored information comprises at least an image of the one or more images. 13. The method of any one of claims 1-12, wherein causing autonomous execution of at least one sprinter response action of the one or more sprinter response actions further comprises: causing at least one of overhead announcements via a speaker associated with the security system or a visual security cue via a light emitting device. 14. An apparatus for automated sprinter detection, comprising: one or more memories; and one or more processors coupled, in any combination, with the one or more memories and configured, individually or in combination, to: receive one or more images from one or more image sensors associated with a security system; identify a person in the one or more images; calculate an acceleration of the person based on tracking of the person in the one or more images; identify the person as a sprinter based on the acceleration; and cause autonomous execution of one or more sprinter response actions in response to identify the person as the sprinter. 15. The apparatus of claim 14, wherein to calculate the acceleration of the person the one or more processors are further configured to: calculate a distance the person traveled based on the tracking of the person in the one or more images, wherein at least a subset of the one or more images are associated with different frames; and calculate a cumulative speed of the person for the different frames based on the distance traveled by the person, wherein the acceleration is calculated for the different frames based on the cumulative speed and a rolling frame interval. 16. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the rolling frame interval is a predetermined number of frames. 17. The apparatus of any one of claims 15 or 16, wherein the acceleration is a second order derivative of the cumulative speed of the person. 18. The apparatus of any one of claims 15-17, wherein to identify the person as the sprinter based on the acceleration the one or more processors are further configured to: identify a number of peaks of the acceleration within a set of frames; and determine whether the number of peaks of the acceleration satisfies a threshold number of peaks, wherein the person is identified based on the determination that the number of peaks satisfies the threshold number of peaks. 19. The apparatus of claim 18, wherein the one or more processors are further configured to: divide the different frames into different zones; identify a number of zones of the different zones the person traveled through based on tracking of the person in one or more images associated with the different frames; and determine whether the number of zones satisfies a threshold number of zones, wherein the person is identified based further on the determination that the number of zones satisfies the threshold number of zones. 20. The apparatus of any one of claims 14-19, wherein at least a subset of the one or more image sensors are located near an entry point or an exit point of a structure associated with the security system. 21. The apparatus of any one of claims 14-20, wherein to cause autonomous execution of at least one sprinter response action of the one or more sprinter response actions the one or more processors are further configured to: transmit an alert to at least one or more computing devices associated with the security system. 22. The apparatus of any one of claims 14-21, wherein to cause autonomous execution of at least one sprinter response action of the one or more sprinter response actions the one or more processors are further configured to: 

 transmit an alert to one or more computing devices associated with a local security personnel or a law enforcement personnel. 23. The apparatus of claim 22, wherein to transmit the alert the one or more processors are further configured to: identify the person within a person of interest database, wherein the alert indicates information from the person of interest database associated with the person. 24. The apparatus of any one of claims 22 or 23, wherein the one or more processors are further configured to: store information associated with tracking of the person in the one or more images in a data storage unit associated with security system. 25. The apparatus of claim 24, wherein the stored information comprises at least an image of the one or more images. 26. The apparatus of any one of claims 14-25, wherein to cause autonomous execution of at least one sprinter response action of the one or more sprinter response actions the one or more processors are further configured to: cause at least one of overhead announcements via a speaker associated with the security system or a visual security cue via a light emitting device. 27. A non-transitory, computer-readable medium comprising computer executable code, the code when executed by one or more processors causes the one or more processors to, individually or in combination, perform an operation for automated sprinter detection, wherein the operation comprises: receiving one or more images from one or more image sensors associated with a security system; identifying a person in the one or more images; calculating an acceleration of the person based on tracking of the person in the one or more images; identifying the person as a sprinter based on the acceleration; and causing autonomous execution of one or more sprinter response actions in response to identifying the person as the sprinter. 28. The non-transitory, computer-readable medium of claim 27, wherein calculating the acceleration of the person further comprises: calculating a distance the person traveled based on the tracking of the person in the one or more images, wherein at least a subset of the one or more images are associated with different frames; and calculating a cumulative speed of the person for the different frames based on the distance traveled by the person, wherein the acceleration is calculated for the different frames based on the cumulative speed and a rolling frame interval. 29. The non-transitory, computer-readable medium of claim 28, wherein the rolling frame interval is a predetermined number of frames. 30. The non-transitory, computer-readable medium of claim of any one of claims 28 or 29, wherein the acceleration is a second order derivative of the cumulative speed of the person.
